message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Objective</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>To undertake a systematic review of reviews of the prevalence of symptoms and signs of COVID-19 in those aged under 20 years?</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Design</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Narrative systematic review of reviews. PubMed, medRxiv, Europe PMC and COVID-19 Living Evidence Database were searched on 9 October 2020.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Setting</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>All settings, including hospitalised and community settings.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Patients</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>CYP under age 20 years with laboratory-proven COVID-19.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Study review, data extraction and quality</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Potentially eligible articles were reviewed on title and abstract by one reviewer. Quality was assessed using the modified AMSTARS criteria and data were extracted from included studies by two reviewers.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Main outcome measures</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Prevalence of symptoms and signs of COVID-19</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>1325 studies were identified and 18 reviews were included. Eight were high quality, 7 medium and 3 low quality. All reviews were dominated by studies of hospitalised children. The proportion who were asymptomatic ranged from 14.6 to 42%. Fever and cough were the commonest symptoms; proportions with fever ranged from 46 to 64.2% and with cough from 32 to 55.9%. All other symptoms or signs including rhinorrhoea, sore throat, headache, fatigue/myalgia and gastrointestinal symptoms including diarrhoea and vomiting are infrequent, occurring in less than 10-20%.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusions</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Fever and cough are the most common symptoms in CYP with COVID-19, with other symptoms infrequent. Further research on symptoms in community samples are needed to inform pragmatic identification and testing programmes for CYP.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Systematic review of reviews of symptoms and signs of COVID-19 in children and adolescents
